violations.text: Pot roast which was prepared on site, date marked 3/22 was observed held beyond the 7 days. Food items exceeding the 7 days shall be discarded. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

Wood is used in the construction of the food shelves in the basement area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025
The condensate from the walk-in cooler is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Ice machine was taken out of service and cleaned at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025

Milk, cream, and yogurt were observed held at 44-46 degrees Fahrenheit in beverage refrigerator. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in there since the day prior. Yogurt was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Milk and cream were relocated to another refrigeration unit to cool. Refrigerator was taken out of service at time of inspection. Establishment must not use the refrigerator to store time/temperature control for safety foods unless it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025

Wood is used in the construction of the food shelves in the basement area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025

The ice machine is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025
The condensate from the walk-in cooler is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2025

Wood is used in the construction of food storage shelves in the basement area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the sanitizing buckets. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the rack above the knives and plastic shelves in the basement have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023
The condenser in the walk-in cooler is in poor repair (leaking into a container). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023
